label_notes: STEMI presentation at the ED. The billable exam-level code depends on RAMQ time/complexity rules (P.G. 2.2.9, 'examen' vs 'consultation' vs urgence supplements) that can't be determined from transcript content alone — candidate retrieval for this note is also weak (see label_notes on note 2), so treat any candidate list for this transcript as unreliable until retrieval is improved.

Best-effort guess only, NOT verified by a physician/billing expert: category 'Réparation de plaies (débridement compris)' has size-tiered codes (01320 <1cm, 01322 <2.5cm, 01325 extensive/complex, 01327 per-additional-cm). The note describes a 3.5cm single-layer suture repair, which reads like 01322 (base, covers up to 2.5cm) plus 01327 (supplement for the remaining ~1cm) — but the exact P.C. 13 rule text that governs how the supplement applies isn't available to verify this combination. UPDATE 2026-07-14: candidates_for() previously (before the retrieval.py stemming fix) never surfaced 01320/01322/01327 at all for this transcript, because the reference table's own text says 'plaies' (plural) while the transcript says 'plaie' (singular) and the tokenizer didn't stem — those are now unified via French stemming (see app/ramq/retrieval.py) and all three codes appear in the candidate list. Retrieval is no longer the blocker for this case; the code selection itself (01322+01327 vs. some other combination) still needs physician verification.

Pediatric appendicitis with surgical consult/admission. Which party bills what (the seeing ED physician vs. the admitting surgical service) and which exam-level code applies isn't determinable from the transcript without RAMQ billing expertise.

Routine chronic-disease follow-up (T2D + HTA) in a family clinic. Likely maps to a standard 'Examen (patients de moins de 70 ans)' visit code (e.g. 09092/15192 showed up as BM25 candidates) but which specific code/context applies (cabinet vs. GMF, inscribed patient rules, etc.) needs a physician/billing expert to confirm rather than a guess from a non-expert.

New-ish complaint (fatigue, possible depression) with PHQ-9 done, no medication started, follow-up in 2 weeks. Could be a standard exam or a more involved 'consultation' code (e.g. 00061/15656 showed up as BM25 candidates) — the distinction matters for billing and needs a physician/billing expert to confirm, not a guess.
